Oakfield Community Garden — Plot-Holder Rules

All plot-holders must follow these rules:
1. Each plot must be actively used. Plots left unplanted for more than 8 weeks may be reassigned.
2. Plot-holders are responsible for weeding and maintaining a clear boundary between plots.
3. Water usage is limited to 30 minutes per plot per week.
4. Pesticides and herbicides are not permitted.
5. Produce grown in the garden is for personal use only and may not be sold.

TravelSafe Insurance — What's Covered

Your TravelSafe policy covers the following:
- Trip cancellation due to sudden illness or injury (with doctor's certificate)
- Lost or stolen baggage (up to ¥100,000 per claim)
- Emergency medical treatment abroad
- Flight delays of 4 hours or more (¥5,000 compensation)

Not covered:
- Cancellations due to change of mind or personal preference
- Items left unattended in public places
- Pre-existing medical conditions

Harbor Arts Theatre — Ticket Refund Policy

Refunds are available under the following conditions:
- The performance is cancelled by the theatre.
- You request a refund at least 48 hours before the performance.

Refunds will NOT be issued if:
- The request is made less than 48 hours before the performance.
- The ticket has already been used.
- The ticket was purchased through a third-party seller.

To request a refund, present your original ticket at the box office or contact us at tickets@harborarts.jp

Nova Solutions — Our Commitment to the Environment

At Nova Solutions, we believe businesses have a responsibility to protect our planet. Starting this year, all our office supplies are sourced from certified sustainable providers. We have reduced paper use by 40% through digital workflows, and our headquarters now runs entirely on renewable energy.

We invite our employees, clients, and partners to join us in this commitment. Small changes — like choosing reusable cups and printing double-sided — can make a big difference.

To learn more about our sustainability goals, visit our website or speak with your team leader.

We are excited to announce that our school library will be undergoing renovations during the summer break. The new library will feature expanded study spaces, updated computer terminals, and a dedicated reading lounge for students.

Westbridge High School Administration

Pinewood Apartments — Residents' Notice

Please be informed of the following upcoming changes:

1. The main entrance intercom system will be replaced on Thursday, June 19 (9:00 a.m.–3:00 p.m.). A temporary buzzer will be in place during this time.

2. The rooftop terrace will be closed for cleaning every Tuesday from 8:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m.

3. All residents are reminded that bicycles must be stored in the designated basement bike room only. Bicycles found in hallways or near entrances will be removed.

Thank you for your cooperation.
Pinewood Apartments Management

Helping Hands Charity Run 2026

Lace up your shoes and make a difference!

Join us for the annual 5K and 10K charity run supporting local youth sports programs.

Date: Sunday, September 13
Start time: 8:00 a.m. (10K) / 9:30 a.m. (5K)
Location: Greenfield Sports Complex

Registration fee:
  5K: ¥2,000 | 10K: ¥3,000
  (All proceeds go directly to the charity.)

Sign up by September 1 at www.helpinghandsrun.jp
T-shirts are available for pre-order (¥1,500) until August 20.

Luminos Online Store — Return Policy

We accept returns within 30 days of delivery under these conditions:
- Item must be unused and in its original packaging.
- Proof of purchase (order confirmation email) is required.
- Return shipping costs are the customer's responsibility unless the item is defective.

The following items cannot be returned:
- Digital downloads and software licenses
- Customized or personalized products
- Items marked "Final Sale" at time of purchase
---

---
Keepin' Active — City Health Campaign

Did you know that just 30 minutes of moderate exercise a day can lower your risk of heart disease, improve your mood, and boost your energy levels?

This summer, the City of Maplewood is launching a series of free outdoor fitness classes every Saturday morning at Riverside Park. Whether you are a beginner or already active, there is a class for you.

All classes are free and open to residents of all ages. No registration required — just show up!

For the full schedule, visit www.maplewoodhealth.gov or pick up a flyer at City Hall.
